stem: The function g(w) = 4w² gives the area of a rectangle, in square meters (m²), if its width is w m and its length is 4 times its width. Which of the following is the best interpretation of g(9) = 324?

answerOptions.content: If the width of the rectangle is 9 m, then the length of the rectangle is 324 m.
If the width of the rectangle is 324 m, then the length of the rectangle is 9 m.
If the width of the rectangle is 9 m, then the area of the rectangle is 324 m².
If the width of the rectangle is 324 m, then the area of the rectangle is 9 m².

rationale: Choice C is correct. g(w) is the area in m² when the width is w m, so g(9) = 324 means a width of 9 m yields an area of 324 m². Choices A, B, and D misinterpret the input and output.
